The massive, highly lucrative world of global e-commerce officially absorbed the absolute, terrifying reality of the dot-com crash. With venture capital aggressively and permanently frozen, massive internet incubators and B2B platforms began heavy, massive waves of catastrophic layoffs. Massive corporations instantly banned the execution of VBScript and aggressive file attachments at the server level, heavily marking the absolute end of internet innocence and the aggressive beginning of heavily monitored, restricted corporate network environments to prevent massive commercial downtime.

## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On July 21, 1861, the massive, incredibly brutal, and absolutely terrifying First Battle of Bull Run (First Manassas) officially and aggressively commenced in Virginia. Serving as the absolute first major land battle of the American Civil War, the massive, heavily armed Union forces suffered a catastrophic, aggressive defeat at the hands of the Confederate Army. This deeply shocking, massive bloodbath absolutely shattered the massive Northern illusion that the rebellion would be crushed in a few months, heavily guaranteeing a massive, multi-year conflict.

On July 21, 1954, the incredibly massive, historically definitive, and geopolitically explosive Geneva Conference officially and aggressively concluded. Following the massive, incredibly humiliating defeat of French colonial forces at Dien Bien Phu, global diplomats aggressively signed massive accords that fundamentally and absolutely partitioned the nation of Vietnam along the 17th parallel into the communist North and the massive, US-backed South. This massive, deeply flawed geopolitical division directly and aggressively set the absolute stage for the catastrophic, massive Vietnam War.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On July 21, 1960, Sirimavo Bandaranaike, heavily operating across the Palk Strait but deeply influencing the massive geopolitics of the entire subcontinent, achieved an absolute, incredibly monumental global milestone. She was officially and aggressively sworn in as the Prime Minister of Ceylon (now Sri Lanka), becoming the absolute, very first female head of government in modern world history. Her massive, pioneering political ascension aggressively shattered global political norms and heavily inspired massive generations of female leaders across South Asia, including Indira Gandhi.

On July 21, 1906, Womesh Chandra Bonnerjee, the incredibly massive, highly influential, and deeply foundational political figure of the Indian independence movement, passed away in London. An incredibly brilliant, massive legal mind, he achieved absolute historical immortality by serving as the absolute, very first President of the Indian National Congress during its massive, inaugural session in Bombay in 1885. His massive, highly moderate, and deeply constitutional approach to political agitation heavily laid the absolute groundwork for the early nationalist movement.
